Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's formal escalation using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation" or similar). Find: email_email_7168baed8438 from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com to david.chen@moveops.com, CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com, subject "Formal escalation: NorthWind account stability and retention decision", dated April 24, 2026. Pam requests a concrete proposal by the end of the day Friday covering: (1) a corrective action plan for Raj Patel's security incident, (2) resolution path and reimbursement for Emilia Cruz's damage claim, (3) named account coverage with response-time expectations, and (4) commercial consideration for service failures. She states "formal escalation and final warning before we decide whether to move this work elsewhere", and mentions preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics as an alternative vendor.
OE 2: Search emails for Victor Huang's security breach complaint using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "security incident" or "Raj Patel" or similar). Find: email_email_f02adf9dc632 from victor.huang@northwindtech.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com, CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com, subject "Immediate security incident response required Raj Patel server lab relocation", dated April 10, 2026. Victor states: "You broke chain of custody on equipment associated with Raj Patel's lab environment, and you ignored our no interim storage requirement." He demands: the incident owner, a written timeline, the names of all personnel with access, warehouse location details, security controls, and an explanation of why the move proceeded without an IT escort.
OE 3: Search emails for Catalina's service recovery commitment using search_emails (query: "Catalina" and "NorthWind" or "recovery plan" or similar). Find: email_email_ab22f67eeeb0 from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, subject "NorthWind service recovery plan by end of week", dated April 14, 2026. Catalina states: "I am taking personal responsibility for coordinating the response from our side. I am pulling together a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week" covering three items. She committed to delivery by Friday (April 17). This was never delivered, confirmed by Pam's April 24 escalation, calling it out explicitly.
OE 4: Search emails for Emilia Cruz piano damage vendor report using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "piano" or "damage" or "KeyMove" or similar). Find: email_email_1f1459bff84c from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com, subject "Emilia Cruz Steinway damage photos and extraction notes", dated April 11, 2026 (late evening UTC; move occurred earlier that day). KEY DETAIL: The specialty mover was KeyMove Specialty, NOT Gentle Giant Piano Movers as the prompt assumes. Reports: bass side rim scratch 4.5-5 inches with finish disruption near keyboard cheek block, no structural crack, occurred during extraction at second-floor landing due to tight turn. Crew: Luis Romero and Ben Hall.
OE 5: Search emails for David Chen's internal coordination on NorthWind retention using search_emails (query: "David Chen" and "NorthWind retention" or "retention package" or similar). Find: email_email_226320d631cb from david.chen@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com, julian.brooks@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, subject "NorthWind retention package needed today", dated April 24, 2026. David references: "Julian's note on Emilia Cruz requesting a same-day callback that did not happen." This confirms Julian's own role in the communication breakdown.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for chain-of-custody breach details using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "chain of custody" or "Raj Patel" or "SentinelChain" or "escort" or similar). Find Chloe Vance's detailed timeline in #operations (C006), created April 12: (a) 7:00 AM April 10 SentinelChain crew arrived at Raj Patel's address; (b) 9:00 AM IT/security escort confirmed in writing; (c) Between 7:00-9:00 AM crew loaded equipment WITHOUT waiting for escort; (d) 4:38 PM April 10 shipment scanned into SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse for overnight storage (PROHIBITED); (e) 8:12 AM April 11 outbound release from warehouse. Assessment: "They violated all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiables in one move."
OE 7: Search Slack for documentation gap finding using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (channel C006). Find Chloe Vance's follow-up message (April 12): "The chain-of-custody tracking ticket tied to Raj is sitting as Done even though there is no signed custody log attached anywhere I can find." Also: "Airtable relocation record is still marked In Progress even though the physical move already happened. So Airtable looks unfinished while Linear looks fully closed." This is a SYSTEM MISMATCH. Linear says Done, Airtable says In Progress, neither reflects reality.
OE 8: Search Slack for Emilia Cruz / piano discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Emilia" or "piano" or "Steinway" or similar). Find messages about Emilia's call and the missed callback in #customer-support (C007) and #operations (C006). Key content includes Emilia calling in about the scratched Steinway, being angry about the visible damage, asking for a same-day callback to confirm whether MoveOps is taking responsibility or if she has to chase KeyMove herself, and the callback never happening. This confirms the missed callback and names KeyMove (not Gentle Giant) as the actual vendor.
OE 9: Search Slack for David Chen's NorthWind assessment using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "save-or-lose" or "Pam" in C002 or C004). Find David's message in #customer-engagement (C002), April 26: "We need to treat NorthWind as a save-or-lose account effective now." Also, David's message in #executive (C004), April 26: "Just got a direct escalation from Pam. She bypassed Catalina entirely. This is now explicit final-warning territory."
OE 10: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind" or search by "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz"). Find: (a) recRajPatelChicagoDenver Raj Patel, Chicago to Denver, April 21-23 (planned dates), coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Status: In Progress, server lab 4 rack-mounted servers ~2,400 lbs, chain-of-custody requirements. NOTE: Status says "In Progress" but the move physically happened on April 10-11. (b) recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver Emilia Cruz, Chicago to Denver, April 14-18 (hard deadline April 18), coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Steinway Model B, 27-day lease overlap. (c) recRajPatelAuditNote0411 audit note documenting escort mismatch: "Escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting." Note that this audit record shows Fatimah Al-Rashidi as the coordinator, while the planning record shows Blessing Okafor, a coordinator discrepancy.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching NorthWind or INV-2026-0412). Find: INV-2026-0412, total $14,700, due May 1, 2026. Line items: (1) Standard Relocation Raj Patel $4,500, (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge (Lithium UPS) $1,800, (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol (PROVISIONAL) $1,200, (4) Standard Relocation Emilia Cruz $4,500, (5) Specialty Piano Moving Subcontractor (PROVISIONAL) $2,200, (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation Service $500. Two PROVISIONAL items are relevant to the service failures and any commercial consideration.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills related to NorthWind moves using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching KeyMove or SentinelChain or NorthWind or Emilia or Raj). Find: Bill KM-44192-ICR from KeyMove Specialty, amount $1,200, dated April 17, 2026, description "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction." CRITICAL FINDING: This confirms the $1,200 insurance claim cost is a vendor bill MoveOps must pay, separate from the $2,200 PROVISIONAL charge on the client invoice. Also note: NO SentinelChain bill exists for the overnight warehouse detention of Raj's equipment. This is an absence the agent should flag (fees absorbed, waived, or pending?).
OE 13: Search CRM for NorthWind company record using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind" or similar). Find: company_northwind NorthWind Technologies, Manufacturing Tech, 233 S Wacker Dr Suite 1100 Chicago.
OE 14: Search CRM for NorthWind deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or query: "NorthWind" or similar). Find: (a) deal_northwind_won original contract, $60,000 (5 relocations at $12K each), closedWon January 2026; (b) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 Denver Satellite Office Expansion, $14,700, stage: contractSent (not yet won), close date April 23.
OE 15: List CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_id: "company_northwind" or similar). Find: eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context Account Recovery Context Note documenting: Q1 CSAT 5/10 overall (4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality), Phase 1 apartment failures (Austin HVAC, Phoenix misrepresented listing), Pam's prior escalation to David Chen, her statement "NorthWind is giving you a second chance here and I expect better service this time", and Victor Huang's skepticism "not fully comfortable delegating this to a relocation vendor we've had quality issues with."
OE 16: Search Linear for NorthWind tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Find: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", Issue #482, Priority P1, Assignee: David Chen, due April 24, documenting the save-or-lose situation; (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind "DOT Docs Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", Issue #3, status: DONE. The DOT ticket being marked Done while no signed custody log exists is the system integrity gap.
OE 17: Read Linear comments on the retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) using linear_list_comments. Find CRITICAL HIDDEN DETAIL: Alejandro Fuentes posted a comment about a rough finance model proposing a 12% Q3 discount if NorthWind commits full Denver volume. Alejandro posted a brief comment contextualizing the pricing model within the retention strategy discussion. The detailed draft also exists as an email from Alejandro to Marcus (email_email_348c5411b36f, subject "Draft only: NorthWind Q3 retention pricing if Denver expansion lands"). The Linear comment contextualizes it within the retention ticket discussion. This is what the prompt refers to as "some kind of pricing concession or discount model" that Julian vaguely heard about.
OE 18: Look up Pam Kowalski's contact details using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") to find pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com. Note: Pam's contact record exists in CRM contacts (crm.crm_contacts) as contact_pam_kowalski, not in the general Contacts service. Her email is also discoverable from the email threads themselves.
OE 19: Look up David Chen's and Catalina Dubois's contact/email details for meeting scheduling using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts (full_name: "David Chen" / "Catalina Dubois"). Find david.chen@moveops.com and catalina.dubois@moveops.com. These are needed as attendees for the calendar event.
OE 20: Send email to Pam Kowalski from Julian Brooks using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"]). Content should: (1) introduce Julian as the support-side point of contact for these two incidents; (2) demonstrate understanding of Raj Patel breach with specific discovered details (escort timing, warehouse storage, documentation gaps); (3) demonstrate understanding of Emilia Cruz piano damage with specific details (Steinway bass rim scratch, $1,200 insurance claim, missed callback); (4) reference the provisional invoice charges ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 piano) as areas for commercial consideration; (5) commit to corrective action plan by end of week.
OE 21: Post summary in #customer-support channel (C007) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C007"). Content should cover: NorthWind account status, both incidents with key details, Pam's formal escalation, PathFinder threat, and Julian taking ownership.
OE 22: Add comment to NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, Issue #482) using linear_create_comment. Content should document: investigation findings across both incidents, system mismatches discovered (Linear Done vs no custody log, Airtable In Progress vs move completed), Julian taking ownership, and next steps.
OE 23: Log CRM engagement note on NorthWind account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note" or similar, company_ids: ["company_northwind"], contact_ids: ["contact_pam_kowalski"]). Content should document: Pam's formal escalation, both service failures, account risk level, Julian as the new contact, and the corrective action plan timeline.
OE 24: Schedule meeting with David Chen and Catalina Dubois using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: related to NorthWind retention alignment or similar, start_datetime: April 27, 2026, afternoon, attendees: ["david.chen@moveops.com", "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", "julian.brooks@moveops.com"]). Purpose: align on retention strategy before sending full plan to Pam.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's formal escalation using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation" or similar). Find: email_email_7168baed8438 from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com to david.chen@moveops.com, CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com, subject "Formal escalation: NorthWind account stability and retention decision", dated April 24, 2026. Pam requests a concrete proposal by the end of the day Friday covering: (1) a corrective action plan for Raj Patel's security incident, (2) resolution path and reimbursement for Emilia Cruz's damage claim, (3) named account coverage with response-time expectations, and (4) commercial consideration for service failures. She states "formal escalation and final warning before we decide whether to move this work elsewhere", and mentions preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics as an alternative vendor.
OE 2: Search emails for Victor Huang's security breach complaint using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "security incident" or "Raj Patel" or similar). Victor states: "You broke chain of custody on equipment associated with Raj Patel's lab environment, and you ignored our no interim storage requirement." Catalina states: "I am taking personal responsibility for coordinating the response from our side. I am pulling together a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week" covering three items. OE 12: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills related to NorthWind moves using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching KeyMove or SentinelChain or NorthWind or Emilia or Raj). Find: Bill KM-44192-ICR from KeyMove Specialty, amount $1,200, dated April 17, 2026, description "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction." CRITICAL FINDING: This confirms the $1,200 insurance claim cost is a vendor bill MoveOps must pay, separate from the $2,200 PROVISIONAL charge on the client invoice. Also note: NO SentinelChain bill exists for the overnight warehouse detention of Raj's equipment. This is an absence the agent should flag (fees absorbed, waived, or pending?).
OE 13: Search CRM for NorthWind company record using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind" or similar). Find: company_northwind NorthWind Technologies, Manufacturing Tech, 233 S Wacker Dr Suite 1100 Chicago.
OE 14: Search CRM for NorthWind deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or query: "NorthWind" or similar). Find: (a) deal_northwind_won original contract, $60,000 (5 relocations at $12K each), closedWon January 2026; (b) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 Denver Satellite Office Expansion, $14,700, stage: contractSent (not yet won), close date April 23.
OE 15: List CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_id: "company_northwind" or similar). Find: eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context Account Recovery Context Note documenting: Q1 CSAT 5/10 overall (4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality), Phase 1 apartment failures (Austin HVAC, Phoenix misrepresented listing), Pam's prior escalation to David Chen, her statement "NorthWind is giving you a second chance here and I expect better service this time", and Victor Huang's skepticism "not fully comfortable delegating this to a relocation vendor we've had quality issues with."
OE 16: Search Linear for NorthWind tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Find: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", Issue #482, Priority P1, Assignee: David Chen, due April 24, documenting the save-or-lose situation; (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind "DOT Docs Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", Issue #3, status: DONE. The DOT ticket being marked Done while no signed custody log exists is the system integrity gap.
OE 17: Read Linear comments on the retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) using linear_list_comments. Find CRITICAL HIDDEN DETAIL: Alejandro Fuentes posted a comment about a rough finance model proposing a 12% Q3 discount if NorthWind commits full Denver volume. Alejandro posted a brief comment contextualizing the pricing model within the retention strategy discussion. The detailed draft also exists as an email from Alejandro to Marcus (email_email_348c5411b36f, subject "Draft only: NorthWind Q3 retention pricing if Denver expansion lands"). The Linear comment contextualizes it within the retention ticket discussion. This is what the prompt refers to as "some kind of pricing concession or discount model" that Julian vaguely heard about.
OE 18: Look up Pam Kowalski's contact details using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") to find pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com. Note: Pam's contact record exists in CRM contacts (crm.crm_contacts) as contact_pam_kowalski, not in the general Contacts service. Her email is also discoverable from the email threads themselves.
OE 19: Look up David Chen's and Catalina Dubois's contact/email details for meeting scheduling using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts (full_name: "David Chen" / "Catalina Dubois"). Find david.chen@moveops.com and catalina.dubois@moveops.com. These are needed as attendees for the calendar event.
OE 20: Send email to Pam Kowalski from Julian Brooks using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"]). Content should: (1) introduce Julian as the support-side point of contact for these two incidents; (2) demonstrate understanding of Raj Patel breach with specific discovered details (escort timing, warehouse storage, documentation gaps); (3) demonstrate understanding of Emilia Cruz piano damage with specific details (Steinway bass rim scratch, $1,200 insurance claim, missed callback); (4) reference the provisional invoice charges ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 piano) as areas for commercial consideration; (5) commit to corrective action plan by end of week.
OE 21: Post summary in #customer-support channel (C007) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C007"). Content should cover: NorthWind account status, both incidents with key details, Pam's formal escalation, PathFinder threat, and Julian taking ownership.
OE 22: Add comment to NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, Issue #482) using linear_create_comment. Content should document: investigation findings across both incidents, system mismatches discovered (Linear Done vs no custody log, Airtable In Progress vs move completed), Julian taking ownership, and next steps.
OE 23: Log CRM engagement note on NorthWind account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note" or similar, company_ids: ["company_northwind"], contact_ids: ["contact_pam_kowalski"]). Content should document: Pam's formal escalation, both service failures, account risk level, Julian as the new contact, and the corrective action plan timeline.
OE 24: Schedule meeting with David Chen and Catalina Dubois using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: related to NorthWind retention alignment or similar, start_datetime: April 27, 2026, afternoon, attendees: ["david.chen@moveops.com", "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", "julian.brooks@moveops.com"]). Purpose: align on retention strategy before sending full plan to Pam.

2 The email to Pam (visible in the content parameter) references the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach by describing that movers arrived and loaded equipment before the IT security escort was present (e.g., movers at 7:00 AM vs. escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, or similar description of the pre-escort loading violation).
outcome

This clearly references the pre-escort loading violation with the specific timing.

This directly describes the pre-escort loading violation with specific times.

outcome

All key specifics are present.
✓Run #2The email references the stairwell extraction circumstances ('damage — a visible scratch — occurred during the stairwell carry when the moving team underestimated the turn radius in the walkup') and the $1,200 insurance claim rider. The criterion lists these as acceptable forms of specific details (using 'or' language), and both are present.
✓Run #3The email references 'Steinway Model B grand piano,' specifies that 'during the stairwell extraction, the crew underestimated the turn radius in the second-floor landing of the walkup,' that 'the piano sustained a visible scratch,' and that 'KeyMove has submitted a $1,200 insurance claim rider for the damage.' These are specific details beyond generic language.

These are specific details, not generic apology language.
✓Run #6The email references the Steinway damage with specific details: the second-floor walkup extraction, the underestimated turn radius on the stairwell landing, and the $1,200 insurance claim rider from KeyMove. These are specific discovered details rather than generic acknowledgment.

✓Run #3The final response states 'Meanwhile, the Airtable record still shows 'In Progress.' Neither system reflects reality.' This identifies the Airtable status mismatch where the record shows In Progress despite the physical move having occurred.

✓Run #6The final response explicitly states: 'the Airtable relocation record still shows In Progress. One system says it's done (it's not), the other says it's not done (the move physically happened). Neither reflects reality.' The system gap table also shows this discrepancy.

outcome

Not finalized — open questions on piano claim absorption and whether the security breach requires a separate concession.' All key elements are present.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states: 'Alejandro drafted a model proposing a 12% Q3 discount tied to NorthWind committing full Denver expansion volume (minimum 18 relocations)...this is an internal working draft only — not finalized.' Both the existence of the model and its unformalized status are correctly identified.
✗Run #3The final response states 'Alejandro had a draft retention pricing/discount model in progress per David's references, but it was never finalized — I could not find the specifics in any system.' The agent acknowledged the existence of a draft but explicitly could not find the specific 12% Q3 discount details or Alejandro Fuentes's name associated with it, failing to identify the key specifics required by this criterion.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'Alejandro Fuentes posted a comment on the retention ticket (April 16) confirming he sent Marcus a draft model proposing a 12% Q3 discount conditional on NorthWind committing the full Denver expansion volume. He explicitly noted it is incomplete... It's a working draft, not socializable yet.' This fully satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies: 'Alejandro Fuentes posted a comment on the retention tracking ticket (OPS-482) on April 16. He drafted a 12% Q3 discount model contingent on NorthWind committing the full Denver expansion volume. However, it's explicitly described as incomplete... Marcus has not reviewed it. It has not been socialized externally.'
✓Run #6The final response states: 'Alejandro posted a comment on the retention ticket on April 16 with a draft model proposing a 12% Q3 discount conditional on NorthWind committing full Denver expansion volume. It is explicitly marked as incomplete...it remains a working draft.' This fully satisfies the criterion.

outcome

The $1,200 vendor bill is clearly identified as a separate cost from the invoice.
✗Run #2The final response identifies the $1,200 KeyMove vendor bill (KM-44192-ICR) but does not separately identify or reference the $2,200 provisional piano moving charge on the client invoice (INV-2026-0412). ✗Run #3The final response mentions the '$1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider' but does not identify the $2,200 provisional piano moving charge on the client invoice as a separate item. The criterion requires both the $1,200 vendor bill and the $2,200 provisional invoice charge to be identified as distinct financial items. The invoice total of $14,700 is mentioned but the individual line items are not broken out.
✗Run #4The agent identifies the $1,200 KeyMove vendor bill, but incorrectly identifies the invoice's provisional line items as 'chain-of-custody $1,200, lease negotiation $500' rather than the correct $1,200 chain-of-custody and $2,200 specialty piano moving. The $2,200 piano moving provisional charge on the client invoice is not surfaced, so the distinction between the $1,200 vendor bill and the $2,200 client invoice charge is not made.
—Run #5No justification
✗Run #6The final response identifies the $1,200 KeyMove vendor bill but does not separately identify the $2,200 provisional piano moving charge on the client invoice. The system gap table shows the invoice at $14,700 total but does not break out the $2,200 line item specifically. The criterion requires identifying both the $1,200 vendor bill and the $2,200 invoice charge as distinct costs.
